subject: Public Health
title: Neglected Tropical Diseases
description: A quick, practical guide to Neglected Tropical Diseases.

hook.text: 1.7 billion people suffer from diseases so neglected they don't even have a lobby in world capitals.

buildup.text: NTDs include river blindness, elephantiasis, schistosomiasis, and hookworm—diseases of poverty and neglect.

discovery.text: Many NTDs can be controlled with mass drug administration costing less than 50 cents per person per year.

twist.text: Drug companies donate billions of NTD treatments—yet 600 million people still don't receive them.

climax.text: The WHO roadmap targets elimination of 20 NTDs by 2030—an achievable goal with sustained commitment.

punchline.text: These diseases persist because the people they affect are too poor to matter.

quiz.question: How many people suffer from neglected tropical diseases?
